1 Experimental Design

Design a field-based sensing study to capture how people experience urban spaces.

  • Sensor choice: select wearable and portable sensors (e.g., sound meter, air pollutant sensor, heat sensor) based on target exposures and outcomes.
    Health Tracker
    Phone GPS
    Portable Device
  • Site selection: choose representative urban sites (e.g., streets, parks, plazas) aligned with the research question.
    River Corridor
    Green Street
    Dense Built
  • Route planning: define routes and sampling protocols.
2 Data Collection

Collect synchronized field data following approved and standardized procedures.

After IRB approval, we recruit participants for on-site sensing sessions and collect synchronized streams.

3 Urban Feature Extraction

Translate street-level imagery into measurable urban features.

  • Computer vision for feature detection: detect and quantify micro-scale visual features.
7 Analysis & Visualization

Turn sensing into spatial evidence for design and planning.

5&6 Data Cleaning & Fusion

Clean and integrate environmental, mobility, and imagery-derived features into a single linked dataset.

Environment
Air · Sound · Heat
GPS & Mobility
Location · Route
Imagery Features
Visual · Openness
Health Response
Baseline · Stress
Timestamp
Alignment
Fixed-interval
Resampling
Fused Dataset
Time & Space Linked
4 Health/Response Labeling

Derive interpretable response states from wearable signals (optional).

Training set: WESAD
Models: Transformer
→
Model Training
ACC, EDA, BVP, TEMP
→
Model Prediction
BaselineStressfulRelaxed
